panels.title: Top 10 products
panels.description: Best sellers by revenue in the selected range, with units for context.

targets.rawSql: SELECT oi.product_name AS product, SUM(oi.unit_price_cents*oi.quantity)/100.0 AS revenue, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status IN ('paid','processing','shipped','delivered') AND $__timeFilter(o.created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10

SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(AVG(r.rating), 2) AS avg_rating FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON p.id = r.product_id JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id WHERE r.status = 'approved' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

SELECT date_trunc('day', created_at) AS time, CASE WHEN user_id IS NULL THEN 'guest' ELSE 'registered' END AS metric, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM orders WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1
SELECT date_trunc('day', created_at) AS time, COUNT(*) AS reviews FROM reviews WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Correlated: rating vs revenue by category
panels.description: Ratings and revenue disagree in both directions. High rating + low revenue = an under-marketed gem worth promoting. High revenue + mediocre rating = today's income at churn risk tomorrow. Neither signal shows this alone.

targets.rawSql: WITH rev AS (SELECT p.category_id, SUM(oi.unit_price_cents*oi.quantity)/100.0 AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id WHERE o.status IN ('paid','processing','shipped','delivered') GROUP BY 1), rat AS (SELECT p.category_id, AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS reviews FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON p.id = r.product_id WHERE r.status = 'approved' GROUP BY 1) SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(COALESCE(rat.avg_rating, 0), 2) AS avg_rating, COALESCE(rat.reviews, 0) AS reviews, COALESCE(rev.revenue, 0) AS revenue FROM categories c LEFT JOIN rev ON rev.category_id = c.id LEFT JOIN rat ON rat.category_id = c.id WHERE COALESCE(rev.revenue, 0) > 0 OR COALESCE(rat.reviews, 0) > 0 ORDER BY revenue DESC

panels.title: Conversion funnel
panels.description: Product views → add-to-cart → checkout → paid, in the selected range. Views and add-to-carts come from user_activity; checkout and paid from orders.

targets.rawSql: SELECT '1. Product views' AS stage, COUNT(*) AS count FROM user_activity WHERE event_type = 'view' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) UNION ALL SELECT '2. Added to cart', COUNT(*) FROM user_activity WHERE event_type = 'add_to_cart' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) UNION ALL SELECT '3. Checkout started', COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) UNION ALL SELECT '4. Paid', COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status IN ('paid','processing','shipped','delivered') AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Funnel drop-off
panels.description: Share of each stage that survived from the previous one. The biggest drop is where UX work pays off first.

targets.rawSql: WITH stages AS (SELECT 1 AS ord, 'Product views' AS stage, COUNT(*)::float AS count FROM user_activity WHERE event_type = 'view' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) UNION ALL SELECT 2, 'Added to cart', COUNT(*) FROM user_activity WHERE event_type = 'add_to_cart' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) UNION ALL SELECT 3, 'Checkout started', COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) UNION ALL SELECT 4, 'Paid', COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status IN ('paid','processing','shipped','delivered') AND $__timeFilter(created_at)) SELECT stage, count::int AS count, ROUND((count / NULLIF(LAG(count) OVER (ORDER BY ord), 0) * 100)::numeric, 1) AS kept_pct FROM stages ORDER BY ord

panels.title: View → paid conversion (daily)
panels.description: Paid orders as a share of product views per day. A dip here with steady traffic means the shop is losing buyers, not visitors.

targets.rawSql: WITH v AS (SELECT date_trunc('day', occurred_at) AS d, COUNT(*) AS views FROM user_activity WHERE event_type = 'view' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1), p AS (SELECT date_trunc('day', created_at) AS d, COUNT(*) AS paid FROM orders WHERE status IN ('paid','processing','shipped','delivered') AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1) SELECT v.d AS time, COALESCE(p.paid, 0) / NULLIF(v.views, 0)::float AS conversion FROM v LEFT JOIN p ON p.d = v.d ORDER BY 1
